title: Keep the durable file lock as the baseline and harden heartbeat ownership first.
summary: loop-cli-main's portable liveness mechanism is lifecycle persistence, not the socket itself. OUR file lock is the only current guard tied to the shared packet path, so the first backlog item should be an owner-scoped heartbeat driver around refreshLoopLock.
whyItHelps: Fixes false stale reclaim for live long-running loops without changing cross-host semantics or requiring a resident daemon.

title: Add socket-bind only as an optional host-local admission layer.
summary: loop-cli-main binds before state init, which is valuable for same-host double-start races. In OUR runtime it should be recorded as host-local single-flight layered on top of acquireLoopLock, not presented as a replacement lock.
whyItHelps: Stops same-host duplicate starts before reducer/state-log initialization while preserving the durable packet-level claim.

title: Do not default socket-bind until a resident workflow host or supervisor owns the handle.
summary: loop-cli-main's guard is strong because the daemon keeps the server open. OUR current lock adapter exits immediately after acquire, and multiple command YAMLs shell out to that one-shot adapter, so default socket-bind would require a host/supervisor rewrite.
whyItHelps: Prevents a false hardening where the guard is released before the loop body runs.

title: Treat multi-host exclusion as unsolved by socket-bind.
summary: loop-cli-main's IPC boundary is local machine/user. Two machines sharing the same spec folder can each bind their own local socket or named pipe, so lock metadata and UX must distinguish durable packet locking from host-local single-flight.
whyItHelps: Keeps operator claims accurate and avoids treating host-local IPC as shared-storage fencing.

title: Probe stale socket paths before unlinking if socket-bind is ported.
summary: loop-cli-main unlinks the POSIX socket path before binding. A future OUR socket guard should first probe/connect and only remove confirmed-stale paths, because conservative packet recovery and diagnostics matter more than silent takeover.
whyItHelps: Avoids erasing useful evidence or disrupting a still-live local listener during recovery.

summary: Ranked decision: file-lock heartbeat first, optional host-local socket guard second, socket-as-default only with a resident holder, separate design for true multi-host fencing.
newInfoRatioRationale: Prior S3-01 and S3-02 already established heartbeat and socket mechanics; this iteration adds ranked synthesis, host-boundary policy, and command-surface blast-radius mapping.
